title: Nikita Tsoy
text:
I am a fourth-year PhD student at INSAIT, supervised by Nikola Konstantinov and Martin Jaggi. My research focuses on trustworthy machine learning, particularly on strategic incentives in machine learning systems and shortcut learning.
Before INSAIT, I received an MA in Economics from NES and a BS in Physics from MIPT.

Research

Strategic Incentives

Tsoy, N., Kirev, I., Rahimiyazdi, N., & Konstantinov, N. On the Impact of Performative Risk Minimization for Binary Random Variables. ICML. 2025.
Chakarov, D., Tsoy, N., Minchev, K., & Konstantinov, N. Incentivizing Truthful Collaboration in Heterogeneous Federated Learning. OPT Workshop. 2024.
Tsoy, N., Mihalkova, A., Todorova, T. N., & Konstantinov, N. Provable Mutual Benefits from Federated Learning in Privacy-Sensitive Domains. AISTATS. 2024.
Tsoy, N., & Konstantinov, N. Strategic Data Sharing between Competitors. NeurIPS. 2023.



Shortcut Learning

Tsoy, N., & Konstantinov, N. On Measuring Localization of Shortcuts in Deep Networks. SCSL Workshop. 2025.
Tsoy, N., & Konstantinov, N. Simplicity Bias of Two-Layer Networks beyond Linearly Separable Data. ICML. 2024.



Miscellaneous

Campbell, D., Brodeur, A., Dreber, A., Johannesson, M., Kopecky, J., Lusher, L., & Tsoy, N. The Robustness Reproducibility of the American Economic Review. I4R Discussion Paper Series. 2024.
